approval.note: PO decision 2026-06-12: approved as a synthetic/test-only rubric for Slice E1 lab testing. This approval is limited to synthetic fixture testing and does NOT imply production hiring approval. History: derived fixture created 2026-06-11 because the uploaded test-data zip contained no completed rubric for this role (gap G-2, now resolved by this decision). Criteria are taken verbatim-in-substance from role-profile.md section 7 and job-posting.md approved qualifications; scale follows the repo council contract and rub-sample-001 precedent.

criteria_sources: job-posting.md (Approved Required Qualifications / Approved Preferred Qualifications, from Sample-End-to-End-Hiring-Package.md sections 3-4)
structure_sources: policies/screening-rubric-standard.md sections 4-5 (core rubric structure, scoring approach)

anchors.1: No evidence the criterion is met
anchors.5: Direct, specific evidence for all elements

criteria.name: Strategy and program leadership experience
criteria.definition: Significant experience in strategy, program leadership, digital health, healthcare transformation, or related fields, including leading strategic initiatives or workstreams.
criteria.evidence_expectations: Named senior/strategy roles with durations; strategic initiatives led; strategic analyses, recommendations, or planning documents produced.

criteria.name: Complex multi-stakeholder initiative coordination and delivery
criteria.definition: Demonstrated experience coordinating and delivering complex initiatives involving multiple stakeholders (jurisdictions, partners, internal teams).
criteria.evidence_expectations: Named multi-stakeholder initiatives, the candidate's coordinating role, and concrete delivery outcomes; cross-functional or multi-jurisdictional scope.

criteria.name: Written communication and synthesis
criteria.definition: Strong written communication and ability to synthesize complex information into clear recommendations.
criteria.evidence_expectations: Specific document types produced (briefings, strategic analyses, reports, recommendations) and their audiences; the cover letter is admissible as a direct writing sample only.

criteria.name: Public-sector, healthcare, or similarly governed environment experience
criteria.definition: Experience working in public-sector, healthcare, or similarly complex governance environments.
criteria.evidence_expectations: Named public-sector/health-sector organizations or equivalently governed settings and the candidate's role within them.

criteria.name: Digital health and interoperability/standards familiarity
criteria.definition: Familiarity with digital health interoperability initiatives, standards, healthcare information exchange, or implementation guidance.
criteria.evidence_expectations: Named interoperability/standards work (e.g., information exchange initiatives, implementation guidance, conformance) and the candidate's contribution.

criteria.name: Executive briefing experience
criteria.definition: Experience briefing senior leadership or executive stakeholders.
criteria.evidence_expectations: Named executive/senior-leadership audiences and the briefing artifacts or sessions delivered.

unscored_assets.name: Bilingualism (English and French)
unscored_assets.note: Listed as a preferred qualification in the sources but not scored in v1; record evidence neutrally if present.

follow_up_areas_note: Per screening-rubric-standard.md section 4.4, reviewers should record follow-up questions for ambiguous evidence rather than guessing; missing evidence is never invented (BR-013).
human_review_note: Rubric output is advisory decision support only; every evaluation requires human review (decision_support_only: true, human_review_required: true).
